subject: Geology
title: What Is a Mineral?
description: Learn What Is a Mineral? in one minute.

hook.text: Ice is a mineral. Sugar is not. The difference comes down to five strict rules nature must follow.

buildup.text: A mineral must be naturally occurring, inorganic, solid, have a defined chemical formula, and form crystals.

discovery.text: This means coal isn't a mineral (it's organic), and glass isn't either (no crystal structure).

twist.text: But ice counts. Frozen water forms crystals, occurs naturally, and has a fixed formula. It's a true mineral.

climax.text: The definition is strict for a reason. It separates true minerals from the thousands of natural look-alikes.

punchline.text: Five rules decide what Earth calls a mineral.

quiz.question: Which of these is a true mineral?
